docs.title: markdown-checker
docs.text: Markdown link validation reporting tool. It provides a couple of functions to validate relative paths and web URLs.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
You will need the following prerequisites in order to use this library:

  • Python >= 3.9
  • requests

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install markdown-checker

docs.title: 1, 2, 3 - How To
docs.text:
1. Run pip install markdown-checker.
  2. Run markdown-checker -d {src} -f {func} -gu {url}. Replace {src} with the directory you want to analyze, {func} with the available functions like check_broken_paths, {gu} with your contribution guidance full URL.
  3. The output will be displayed in the terminal and in a comment.md file.

docs.title: Advanced Usage
docs.text: To further customize your experience with the Markdown Checker, you can utilize additional command-line interface (CLI) options.

docs.title: Working with List Parameters
docs.text:
Several CLI options accept lists of values (e.g., --skip-domains, --skip-files, --extensions, --tracking-domains, --skip-urls-containing).

How to provide multiple values:

Use comma-separated values without spaces:

markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_urls --skip-domains=example.com,test.com,another-site.org

Important formatting rules: - Values must be separated by commas with no spaces - Do not use square brackets - Do not add spaces after commas - Quotes are optional but not required

Examples of correct and incorrect usage: - ❌ Wrong: --skip-domains=[example.com,test.com] (square brackets not allowed) - ❌ Wrong: --skip-domains=example.com, test.com (spaces not allowed) - ❌ Wrong: --skip-domains example.com test.com (must use = and commas) - ✅ Correct: --skip-domains=example.com,test.com - ✅ Correct: --skip-domains="example.com,test.com" (quotes optional)

docs.title: -d, --dir
docs.text:
• Type: click.Path
  • Description: Path to the root directory to check.
  • Required: Yes

docs.title: -f, --func
docs.text:
• Type: click.Choice
  • Description: Function to be executed.
  • Choices:
  • check_broken_paths
  • check_broken_urls
  • check_paths_tracking
  • check_urls_tracking
  • check_urls_locale
  • Required: Yes

docs.title: -ext, --extensions
docs.text:
• Type: list[str]
  • Description: File extensions to filter the files.
  • Default:
  • .md
  • .ipynb
  • Required: No
  • Usage Examples: ```bash # Single value markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_paths --extensions=.md

# Multiple values (comma-separated, no spaces) markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_paths --extensions=.md,.ipynb,.txt ```

docs.title: -td, --tracking-domains
docs.text:
• Type: list[str]
  • Description: List of tracking domains to check.
  • Default:
  • github.com
  • microsoft.com
  • visualstudio.com
  • aka.ms
  • azure.com
  • Required: No
  • Usage Examples: ```bash # Single value markdown-checker -d . -f check_urls_tracking --tracking-domains=github.com

# Multiple values (comma-separated, no spaces) markdown-checker -d . -f check_urls_tracking --tracking-domains=github.com,microsoft.com,example.com ```

docs.title: -sf, --skip-files
docs.text:
• Type: list[str]
  • Description: List of file names to skip check.
  • Default:
  • CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
  • SECURITY.md
  • Required: No
  • Usage Examples: ```bash # Single value markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_paths --skip-files=README.md

# Multiple values (comma-separated, no spaces) markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_paths --skip-files=README.md,CHANGELOG.md,LICENSE.md ```

docs.title: -sd, --skip-domains
docs.text:
• Type: list[str]
  • Description: List of domains to skip checking.
  • Default: []
  • Required: No
  • Usage Examples: ```bash # Single value markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_urls --skip-domains=example.com

# Multiple values (comma-separated, no spaces) markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_urls --skip-domains=example.com,test.com,another-site.org ```

docs.title: -suc, --skip-urls-containing
docs.text:
• Type: list[str]
  • Description: List of strings to skip checking if their urls are working or not.
  • Default:
  • https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog
  • video-embed.html
  • Required: No
  • Usage Examples: ```bash # Single value markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_urls --skip-urls-containing=/embed/

# Multiple values (comma-separated, no spaces) markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_urls --skip-urls-containing=/embed/,/preview/,video-embed.html ```

docs.title: -gu, --guide-url
docs.text:
• Type: str
  • Description: Full URL of your contributing guide.
  • Required: No

docs.title: -to, --timeout
docs.text:
• Type: Click.IntRange
  • Description: Timeout in seconds for the requests before retrying.
  • Default: 15
  • Range: 0-50
  • Required: No

docs.title: -rt, --retries
docs.text:
• Type: Click.IntRange
  • Description: Number of retries for the requests before flagging a url as broken.
  • Default: 3
  • Range: 0-10
  • Required: No

docs.title: -o, --output-file-name
docs.text:
• Type: str
  • Description: Name of the output file.
  • Default: comment
  • Required: No

docs.title: SRC ...
docs.text:
• Type: click.Path
  • Description: Source files or directories to check.
  • Required: No

docs.title: --version
docs.text:
• Type: bool
  • Description: Show the version and exit.
  • Required: No

docs.title: --help
docs.text:
• Type: bool
  • Description: Show the help message and exit.
  • Required: No

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
• Main
  • Urls
  • Paths
  • Markdown Link Base
  • Utilities

docs.title: Using markdown-checker in GitHub Actions
docs.text:
You can run this tool within a GitHub workflow using the action-check-markdown GitHub action.

The action will automatically post the output of the tool to your GitHub pull request as a comment.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The library provides the following functions:

Usage:

  • check_broken_paths
  • check_broken_urls
  • check_urls_locale
  • check_paths_tracking
  • check_urls_tracking

docs.title: check_broken_paths
docs.text:
This function ensures that any relative path in your files are working.

Example:

markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_paths -gu https://github.com/john0isaac/markdown-checker/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: check_broken_urls
docs.text:
This function ensures that any web URL in your files is working and returning 200 status code.

Example:

markdown-checker -d . -f check_broken_urls -gu https://github.com/john0isaac/markdown-checker/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: check_urls_locale
docs.text:
This function checks if country specific locale is present in URLs.

Example:

markdown-checker -d . -f check_urls_locale -gu https://github.com/john0isaac/markdown-checker/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: check_paths_tracking
docs.text:
This function ensures that any relative path has tracking in it.

Example:

markdown-checker -d . -f check_paths_tracking -gu https://github.com/john0isaac/markdown-checker/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: check_urls_tracking
docs.text:
This function ensures that any URL has tracking in it.

Example:

markdown-checker -d . -f check_urls_tracking -gu https://github.com/john0isaac/markdown-checker/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: Want to do more? Check out the Advanced Usage page
Main
docs.text: Module providing automatic checks functionality to markdown files following some Guidelines

docs.title: ListOfStrings
docs.text:
Bases: Option

Helper class to parse a list of strings from the command line.

Ref: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47631914/how-to-pass-several-list-of-arguments-to-click-option

docs.title: detect_issues(func, file_path, skip_urls_containing, skip_domains, tracking_domains, timeout, retries)
docs.text:
Function to detect issues in the markdown file based on the function

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default func str

Function to be executed

required file_path Path

Path to the markdown file

required skip_urls_containing list[str]

List of urls to skip check

required skip_domains list[str]

List of domains to skip check

required tracking_domains list[str]

List of tracking domains to check

required timeout int

Timeout in seconds for the requests

required retries int

Number of retries for the requests

required

Returns:

Type Description tuple[list[Union[MarkdownPath, MarkdownURL]], int]

Detected issues and links count.

docs.title: spinner(beep=False, disable=False, force=False, stream=sys.stdout)
docs.text:
This function creates a context manager that is used to display a spinner on stdout as long as the context has not exited.

The spinner is created only if stdout is not redirected, or if the spinner is forced using the force parameter.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default beep bool

Beep when spinner finishes.

False disable bool

Hide spinner.

False force bool

Force creation of spinner even when stdout is redirected.

False

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
with spinner():
    do_something()
    do_something_else()
